diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers keep the claim unsupported for the same core reason: the evidence bundle does not establish the specific proposition 'choose_fast_shortcut.' The reason codes are identical, so there is no substantive reasoning difference beyond wording. The citation difference reflects whether to cite near-miss evidence. Option 2 is somewhat more diagnostically adequate on citations because evidence-001 is relevant context showing a fast preference that falls short of proving a fast shortcut choice, whereas option 1 cites nothing. The main divergence is confidence: given that evidence-001 partially overlaps semantically with the claim and could invite overreading, medium confidence is better calibrated than high confidence. Thus the disagreement is primarily confidence calibration, with a minor citation practice difference.
